rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEONISA TRIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Administrator Lauro Trias' Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement. No opposition to the motion was filed. Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement is DENIED. This court entered Order Granting Petition for Approval of Amended Settlement Agreement ("Settlement") in the related matter Conservatorship of Leonisa A. Trias (PCN-19-303154), on November 12, 2021. The parties to the Settlement were Lauro Trias, Ramona Figueira, Lazaro Trias, Jr., Prestina Macias, and Prestina's husband Carlos C. Macias. The court retained jurisdiction under Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 to enforce the terms of the Amended Settlement Agreement. Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 states, "If parties to pending litigation stipulate ... for settlement of the case, or part thereof, the court upon motion, may enter judgment pursuant to the terms of the settlement." The Motion alleges Prestina Trias, aka Prestina Macias, and her husband Carlos C. Macias ("Respondents") are in material breach of the Settlement's terms by refusing to move out of estate real property, 121 Seneca Ave, San Francisco, blocking access to the real property, and obstructing attempts to market and sell the real property. The Motion seeks an order directed to Respondents, "to vacate the property within 30 days upon filing and service of the Notice of Entry of Order" and for the order to be "fully enforceable through this Court, through the San Francisco Sheriff's Department, and through a writ of possession in compliance with this Order." (Motion at 6:20-25.) The Motion asserts the parties to the Settlement mutually understood Respondents moving out of the property after Mrs. Trias' death would be necessary to facilitate sale of the property and satisfaction of estate debts from the sale proceeds. Regardless of the parties' alleged mutual understanding, the terms of the Settlement do not include provisions for Respondents' eviction from the property, through a motion to enforce settlement or otherwise. The terms of the Settlement likewise do not provide for a writ of possession to be issued on a motion to enforce judgment. A court may not add to or make a new term of the settlement agreement without mutual consent of the parties (see, Jones v. World Life Research Institute (1976) 60 Cal.App.3d 836, 840; California State Auto. Assn. Inter-Ins. Bureau v. Superior Ct. (1990) 50 Cal. 3d 658, 664). There is no showing before the court that Respondents have consented to the proposed addition or expansion to the terms of the Settlement. The court cannot enter judgment pursuant to terms that are not part of the Settlement. This ruling in not intended to preclude the Administrator from seeking to regain possession of the real property through appropriate legal processes. Administrator Lauro Trias shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR BUI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Petiton Of Final Distribution If Not Filed A Petition To Approve Report Of Status
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) No inventory and appraisal has been filed since letters were issued to the administrator on 04/23/2024. The filing deadline was 08/23/2024. 2) The administrator has not petitioned for final distribution, nor has the administrator petitioned for approval of a first status report, since letters were issued to the administrator on 04/23/2024. The filing deadline was 04/23/2025. 3) The decedent died on 03/19/2023, more than 2 years ago, but no first interim account covering a 2-year period from the date of death (see LRSF 14.34(A)) has been filed. It is unclear whether the administrator intends to petition for final distribution on waiver of account. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/29/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice of this hearings has been filed. Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The report does not meet the requirements set forth in LRSF 14.35 J. (4) Letter were issues on 4/22/24, not 3/5/24. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/15/25. If the issues are not addressed and no I&A is filed by 7/15/25, an appearance will be required on 7/29/25.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANITA M. GARCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/29/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When did the decedent die? The amended petition shows her date of death as 1/10/17, but Robert H. Peterson III's declaration and the proposed order show 1/11/17. (2) The amended petition was e-filed on 4/29/25. Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (3) If Herbert Garcia died on 2/4/24, he survived the decedent. Who is his successor-in-interest (not necessarily his issue)? (4) When did Irene Figueroa die? "Prior to 2024" does not necessarily mean that she predeceased the decedent. Did she not have a trust or was no estate opened for her? (5) A copy of the lost will must be attached to the proposed order. [LRSF 14.20.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 7/15/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
